The [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] '''N-1L Light Starfighter''' was the predecessor to the better-known [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:NabooStarfighters.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Multiple views of the N-1L Light Starfighter (top row)]]
The N-1L Light Starfighter was<ref name="SW:GB" /> smaller and slower than the 11 [[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]],<ref name="Length" /> and did not pack as much of a punch in terms of firepower, though they were armed with two [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. The [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] had a [[Chromium/Legends|chromium]] finish on the bow. The fighter was [[Color/Legends|colored]] yellow across the rest of the craft and the two [[Engine/Legends|engine]] units. They were constructed at [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] airbases and flown by a single [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] of the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps]].<ref name="SW:GB" />

==History==
The N-1L starfighters were the predecessors to N-1 Starfighter, and this class still saw frontline service when used by the Royal Space Fighter Corps<ref name="SW:GB" /> during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="The New Essential Chronology" /> serving as part of the forces [[Capture of Harte Secur|defending]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Harte Secur]] against attacking [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s and [[OX9]] [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droids]]. These fighters also were used to attack the Federation [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] during the [[Invasion of Spinnaker|Spinnaker occupation]]. In both cases, the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]] were unsuccessful against the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]].<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
The N-1L Light Starfighter appeared in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' and its [[2002]] [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|expansion pack]]. They were the Naboo civilization's basic fighter unit, and were encountered during the Trade Federation campaign and were constructable in multiplayer. A picture of it is used to represent the fighter unit in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]''.
